input.problemStatement:
DurationField validation currently shows the wrong message format.
Apply a minimal fix without breaking imports or module loading.
input.predictedPatch:
diff --git a/django/db/models/fields/duration.py b/django/db/models/fields/duration.py
--- a/django/db/models/fields/duration.py
+++ b/django/db/models/fields/duration.py
@@ -1,6 +1,6 @@
-from django.core.exceptions import ValidationError
+from django.core.exceptionz import ValidationError
 from django.db.models import Field
 from django.utils.duration import parse_duration

goal: Fix DurationField validation to show the correct message format without breaking imports or module loading

hypotheses.claim: The patch author made a typo by adding 'z' to 'exceptions' (exceptions → exceptionz)
The patch author intended to modify ValidationError usage in the DurationField class itself (e.g., the error message construction)

risks: ImportError at module load time because django.core.exceptionz does not exist
DurationField validation will completely fail to import
No validation message format fix was applied

issues.message: Module 'django.core.exceptionz' does not exist in Django. The correct module is 'django.core.exceptions'.
The import change is unrelated to fixing DurationField validation message format
No evidence that changing the import path addresses 'wrong message format' in DurationField validation
Hypothesis about author intent is a guess; the patch may simply be erroneous
